---
title: PCA testing
execute:
 jupyter: math4025
---

 ```{python}
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
from plotnine import *
from pyhere import here
from sklearn.base import BaseEstimator, TransformerMixin
from sklearn.impute import SimpleImputer
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import itertools
from sklearn.decomposition import PCA
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler
from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split
```

```{python}
phot = pd.read_csv(here("data/cleaned", "MIRION_cleaned_all_fluxes.csv"))
phot = phot.drop(columns=['LRATIO', 'T_BOL', 'LM', 'L_BOL', 'MASS', 'DIAM', 'TEMP', 'YB'])

X = phot.drop(columns=['SURF_DENS'])
y = phot['SURF_DENS']

flux_cols = ['F8', 'F12', 'F24', 'F70', 'F160', 'F250', 'F350', 'F500', 'F870', 'F1100']
```

```{python}
imputer = SimpleImputer(strategy='median')
X_imputed = imputer.fit_transform(X)

scaler = StandardScaler()
X_scaled = scaler.fit_transform(X_imputed)

pca = PCA()
pca.fit(X_scaled)
```

```{python}
variance_ratio = pca.explained_variance_ratio_
cumulative_variance = np.cumsum(variance_ratio)

# 5. Create the Scree Plot
plt.figure(figsize=(10, 6))

# Plot individual variance as dots connected by lines
plt.plot(range(1, len(variance_ratio) + 1), variance_ratio, 
         marker='o',          # Adds the dots
         linestyle='-',       # Connects them with a straight line
         color='royalblue', 
         linewidth=2, 
         markersize=8, 
         label='Individual explained variance')

# Formatting the plot
plt.title('Scree Plot: Finding the "Elbow"', fontsize=14)
plt.ylabel('Explained Variance Ratio', fontsize=12)
plt.xlabel('Principal Component Index', fontsize=12)

# Ensure x-axis ticks match the component numbers exactly
plt.xticks(range(1, len(variance_ratio) + 1, 2)) 

plt.grid(axis='both', linestyle='--', alpha=0.6)
plt.legend()
plt.tight_layout()

# Display the plot
plt.show()
```
